content: The intrinsic reward signals used to rank novel states in curiosity-driven RL (ICM/RND) can be repurposed as a dynamic memory pruning metric for SOTA 2024-2025 active learning agents, where only states with high prediction error (the core curiosity signal) are retained in episodic memory to avoid redundant storage. This framework lets active learning agents prioritize storing the most information-rich unexpected experiences rather than just the most reward-relevant ones, cutting memory bloat while improving generalization on unseen tasks.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
